subparts.text: The mean marks in Statistics of 100 examinees in a class was 72. The mean marks of boys was 75 while their number was 70. Find the mean marks of girls in the class. 2.5
Two regression lines of a sample are 𝑥+ 6𝑦= 6 and 3𝑥+ 2𝑦= 10. Find the correlation coefficient 2.5
Two coins are tossed. Find the probability of getting both heads or tails. 2.5
Find the expected number of points to be obtained from the throw of a perfect die. 2.5

Formulate a linear programming problem for maximizing profit with the following conditions: A company produces two products, A and B. The profit per unit for A is ₹40 and for B is ₹50. The production of A and B requires 3 hours and 2 hours of labor respectively. The company has 120 hours of labor available per week. Production must meet the condition that the total units of A and B produced do not exceed 50. 2.5 C03
Find the IBFS using the Northwest Corner Method: D1 D2 D3 Supply S1 8 6 10 15 S2 9 12 13 25 S3 14 9 16 10 Demand 20 15 15 2.5

A decision-maker has three options with the following payoffs : State Option 1 Option 2 Option 3 S1 500 300 400 S2 200 600 300 S3 100 200 700 Using the maximin criterion, determine the best option. 2.5

A project manager has two investment options: i) Invest ₹50,000 with a 60% chance of earning ₹80,000 and a 40% chance of earning ₹20,000. ii) Invest ₹50,000 with a guaranteed return of ₹65,000. 2.5 Which option should the manager choose based on expected monetary value ?

questions.question_text: The following data represents the marks obtained by 6 students in Mathematics and Physics : Student Mathematics Physics A 85 78 B 60 72 C 70 65 D 80 70 E 90 88 F 75 77 Calculate the Karl Pearson correlation coefficient between Mathematics and Physics marks. Find the regression equation of 𝑌 on 𝑋

The following table shows the marks obtained by a group of students in a mathematics test: Marks range Frequency 0-10 4 10-20 8 20-30 15 30-40 12 40-50 6 Construct a histogram for the data. Calculate the mean and the median of the marks.

A factory has three machines 𝐴, 𝐵 and 𝐶 producing 30%, 40%, and 30% of the total output, respectively. The probability that a product from machine 𝐴 is defective is 2%, from 𝐵 is 3%, and from 𝐶 is 4%. A randomly selected product is found to be defective. Find the probability that it was produced by machine 𝐴 Using Bayes’ Rule, determine which machine is most likely to have produced the defective product.

A business uses Monte Carlo simulation to estimate the total profit for a product. The cost of production per unit is ₹100, and the selling price per unit is ₹150. The demand per day follows the probability distribution: Demand Probability 50 0.3 60 0.4 70 0.3 Simulate the total profit for a day using 5 random numbers: 0.12, 0.37, 0.45, 0.65, 0.85. Compute the average profit over these 5 simulations.

The transportation costs, supply at origins, and demand at destinations are as follows: Destination 1 Destination 2 Destination 3 Supply Source 1 4 6 8 20 Source 2 5 8 7 15 Source 3 6 4 5 10 Demand 15 25 5 Find the Initial Basic Feasible Solution using the Vogel’s Approximation Method . Test the solution for optimality.

A company has four tasks to be assigned to four workers. The cost matrix is given below: Worker 1 Worker 2 Worker 3 Worker 4 Task 1 20 25 22 30 Task 2 28 27 30 35 Task 3 26 24 28 32 Task 4 30 32 34 36 Solve the assignment problem using the Hungarian method. Find the minimum total cost of assignment.

A company is evaluating whether to launch a new product. The cost of launching is ₹50,000. If successful, the profit is ₹1,00,000; if unsuccessful, the loss is ₹30,000. The probability of success is 0.6, and failure is 0.4. Alternatively, the company can conduct a market survey costing ₹10,000. The survey has a 70% chance of providing favorable results. Draw the decision tree for this scenario. Calculate the Expected Value of Perfect Information and recommend whether the company should conduct the survey.

A project has the following activities with optimistic , most likely , and pessimistic time estimates : Activity Predecesso r O M P A None 3 6 9 B A 4 5 8 C A 5 6 7 D B,C 2 3 6 E C 3 5 8 Calculate the expected time and variance for each activity. Determine the critical path and the probability of completing the project within 20 days.
